title: fix: include Polar top-ups in quest checks
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds a local `polar_checkout_credits` D1 ledger for historical Polar top-up orders.
- Makes `first_top_up` and `top_up_100` read Stripe plus Polar local credit ledgers.
- Adds regressions for Polar-only first top-up and mixed Stripe/Polar 100-pollen completion.

Validation:
- `npm run decrypt-vars && npx vitest run test/quests.test.ts`
- PR checks are green.
- Simulated production merge with `git merge-tree --write-tree origin/production origin/codex/polar-topup-ledger` with no conflicts.
- `wrangler d1 migrations list DB --remote --env production` reports no migrations to apply after the manual apply.

Backfill state:
- `0031_lucky_alex_power.sql` has already been applied to staging and production D1.
- Production `polar_checkout_credits` has 430 rows for 315 existing D1 users.
- Scratch prod table `polar_checkout_credits_backfill_test_20260626` remains with 431 candidates.
- MeatyChunks maps to one 40-pollen Polar top-up on 2025-11-14.
- No rows were inserted into `stripe_checkout_credits`, and no `user.pack_balance` values were changed.

gist.headline: Credit where credit is due
gist.blurb: We fixed a glitch where Polar top-ups were being ignored by the quest system. Your ledger is now fully synced, so go claim those hard-earned achievements.
gist.summary: Updated the `first_top_up` and `top_up_100` quest checks to read from both Stripe and the newly added `polar_checkout_credits` D1 ledger.
gist.impact: Users who purchased Pollen via Polar will now correctly unlock their top-up achievements, with historical data already backfilled.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and a cute chunky polar bear (representing Polar) looking at a glowing quest board or ledger. The polar bear is handing over a coin to check off a quest.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky friendly polar bear looking at a glowing wooden quest board. The polar bear is handing the bee a shiny gold coin to complete a quest. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and icy blue pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
